cover_caption: Equivariance in EquiTac — when tactile observation is rotated, predicted action rotates consistently
source_title: Residual Rotation Correction using Tactile Equivariance

ai_summary.one_liner: SO(2)-equivariant tactile network for sample-efficient in-hand rotation correction — 90% success from 10 demos
ai_summary.problem: Tactile data collection is expensive; existing visuotactile policies need large datasets to generalize across orientations
ai_summary.solution: Exploit SO(2) equivariance in tactile normal maps via C8-equivariant CNN for residual yaw correction on top of flow matching base policy
ai_summary.key_result: Mean 2.9° angular error (vs 13.5° w/o equivariance); 90% success with 10 demos (baseline: 75% with 60 demos); zero-shot to unseen screw geometries

figures.caption: Equivariance in EquiTac. When the tactile observation is rotated, the predicted action rotates consistently.
figures.caption_ko: EquiTac의 등변성. 촉각 관측이 회전하면 예측된 행동도 일관되게 회전한다.

figures.caption: Overview of real-world object manipulation experiments with four daily-life tasks: drawing on paper, straw classification, flower arrangement, and pouring tea.
figures.caption_ko: 실환경 물체 조작 실험 개요. 4가지 일상 과제: 종이 위 그리기, 빨대 분류, 꽃꽂이, 차 따르기.
